findings.message: Loom skin.css drift (recorded sha384-zggW1d97jHMLqD/TTCHVAamnfTXG7VzcwzpumVWUyo1NUQLRk7kcRjF5If9yp8ST, current sha384-uW8b8ZiHt5vnhURoDCeGX6PIGC6X8Xo0j+xDyoKe8R/3I9G9nT6MZmusq/tKLWwR). Run `forge --sync-loom` to update.

advocacy.why: Loom's canonical skin.css has changed (new design-system tokens, fixed a11y contrast, etc.) but static/loom-skin.css still carries the old hash. The served site is shipping the stale design system
advocacy.substrate_fix: run `forge --sync-loom` to copy the current Loom skin.css bytes + update the marker
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't manually update the marker — only the canonical sync command writes both bytes + hash atomically

findings.message: forge.toml missing [platform] block

advocacy.why: per VERSION_DISCIPLINE.md, every site declares its pinned forge_version + loom_version + crawler_version so upgrades are explicit, not silent
advocacy.substrate_fix:
add `[platform]
forge_version = "<exact>"
loom_version = "<exact>"
crawler_version = "<exact>"` to forge.toml

findings.message: static/stripe.html has no cms/stripe.json source — stale artifact

advocacy.why: the canonical render set is cms/*.json; when a CMS source is deleted the previously-rendered static/<slug>.html lingers and downstream audit phases (sri/tokens/perf_budget/unbuilt_route) re-scan it, producing N derived warns per stale file
advocacy.substrate_fix: either: (a) delete static/stripe.html if the page is intentionally gone, OR (b) restore cms/stripe.json if the page should still ship
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't manually edit static/<slug>.html — Forge regenerates it from cms/<slug>.json on every build with write_canonical=true

findings.message: 20.5K HTML > 20.0K budget — audit blocks / split route

advocacy.why: every kilobyte over budget is one more TLS round-trip the reader's browser has to wait for before paint — the budget is the reader-side latency floor, not an internal lint
advocacy.substrate_fix: HTML over budget at 20.5K: audit blocks / split route
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't raise the budget to silence the warn — every active substrate uses the same defaults so over-budget assets are a real regression, not a config mismatch

findings.message: link → loom-skin.css: integrity mismatch (declared sha384-nconHbAg4HoZWyhejJT//wOah37z9RxZML84LqQdFeho/uP8hkqjYV6Y0sbTXYIQ, expected sha384-K1jbU2kGYxL9xh3jIMh68pMCAFmezKHnlcgPZRmX1FjZeR3BzkBVsAVYgx4ySPUN)

advocacy.why: HTML declares a subresource integrity that does not match the file on disk — either the asset was modified post-build OR the integrity was hand-edited
advocacy.substrate_fix: rebuild the asset cleanly (`forge build`) — the Loom integrity emitter regenerates `sha384-sha384-K1jbU2kGY` from the on-disk content. Do NOT hand-edit the integrity in static/
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't paste integrity hashes from anywhere except `forge build` output

findings.message: [cta-contact] declared but impl_files is empty (PARTIAL — stub)

advocacy.why: a backend with empty impl_files is a partial declaration; phantom_button passes (the slug exists) but at runtime the backend has nowhere to dispatch to
advocacy.substrate_fix: populate impl_files for [cta-contact] with the Rust module path(s) that handle the backend's verbs — OR remove the declaration if the implementation isn't planned

advocacy.substrate_fix: populate impl_files for [view-home] with the Rust module path(s) that handle the backend's verbs — OR remove the declaration if the implementation isn't planned

findings.message: href="/prosper-in-life/" → static/prosper-in-life/ does not exist

advocacy.why: every internal href in shipped HTML must resolve to a file in static/. an unresolved href ships to readers as a 404 — silent in the build, loud in the browser
advocacy.substrate_fix: either: (a) add cms/prosper-in-life/.json so render writes static/prosper-in-life/, OR (b) remove the dead link from the rendering source (CMS body / Loom primitive props)
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't manually `touch static/<route>.html` to make the gate pass — that file is regenerated only from cms/*.json on write_canonical builds and will be flagged as an orphan

findings.message: journey file not at ../PlausiDen-Crawler/journeys/poc.json — runtime audit skipped

advocacy.why: the crawler drives the static/ tree through a typed journey (goto / wait / screenshot / diff steps); without the journey JSON the runtime audit has nothing to execute
advocacy.substrate_fix: add a journey JSON at the expected path or override CRAWLER_JOURNEY env — see PlausiDen-Crawler/journeys/ for the schema
advocacy.anti_pattern: don't hand-edit prior run artifacts to fake a journey run; the crawler emits typed diff.json bytes and forge parses with serde, so synthetic state will fail the deserialize path
